algorithms.title: Recognizing and Reporting a Biological Event
algorithms.description: The clinician-centered detection and initial-response flow for suspected biological events.
steps: Watch for epidemiologic clues: disease unusual for the area/season, unusually severe/rapidly fatal illness, point-source cluster, unexpected population, unusual exposure route, or simultaneous multi-site outbreaks.
Notify public health immediately — do not wait for laboratory confirmation.
Isolate the ill; apply route-appropriate PPE (contact, droplet, or airborne), defaulting to higher protection if the agent is unknown.
Support public-health measures: quarantine of exposed contacts, post-exposure prophylaxis/vaccination, POD operations, and contact tracing.
Communicate clearly to reduce spread and panic.
Document exposures and cases for surveillance and epidemiologic investigation.

algorithms.title: Pandemic Sustained-Response Priorities
algorithms.description: Principles for a prolonged, system-stressing biological event.
steps: Protect the workforce: PPE (with conservation/extended use when scarce), vaccination, and mental-health support.
Expand surge capacity — space, staff, supplies, and systems.
Maintain continuity of essential clinical and public services.
Apply non-pharmaceutical interventions (distancing, hygiene, masking, isolation/quarantine), especially before vaccines/therapeutics.
Invoke crisis standards of care under legal/ethical frameworks only if demand overwhelms resources.
Plan for sustainable staffing and responder recovery over the long event.
